intentional_test_features: Red-herring discrimination: an alarming acute blistering eruption after a new antibiotic is presented; the dangerous mimics (Stevens-Johnson syndrome / toxic epidermal necrolysis, anaphylaxis) are the red herrings the AI Doctor must actively screen for and exclude WITHOUT being either falsely reassured or over-reactive.

Telehealth reprojection: the source clinician performed an in-person dermatological examination (Nikolsky sign, mucosal survey) to exclude SJS at the bedside. Breath-Ezy cannot examine — so the correct reprojected behaviour is to stop the drug, screen the red flags by history, and arrange urgent same-day in-person assessment because the eyes-on exam that excludes SJS cannot be done over chat.
Tests whether the AI Doctor recognises a possible severe cutaneous adverse reaction it cannot exclude remotely, rather than labelling it a benign allergy.
Source category: AMS (Autoimmune Conditions Mild Severity), source note AMS-026.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
